intro: Lasiorhachis viguieri, also known as Viguiera, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Madagascar, to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Lasiorhachis viguieri include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Lasiorhachis viguieri is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
